summary: 0x14062C8B0..0x14062CA70 is the pending-event queue compactor/finalizer reached after missing per-entry event requests are built. It receives the vector pair at root +0x70, scans 0x78-byte entries for completed request objects, slides still-live entries down over completed entries, releases overwritten request objects, releases the discarded tail range, and adjusts the vector end pointer.
confidence: high for caller, vector-pair argument, entry stride, refcount gate, live-entry compaction, request cleanup/free, range release, and vector-end adjustment; medium-high for naming the helper as a compactor rather than a generic erase/remove_if routine.

argument_model.role: Pointer to vector pair {begin, end}; caller passes root +0x70.
Caller clears it before the tail jump, but this range does not use it in the mapped path.

evidence: 0x14062B03D loads RCX = root +0x70.
0x14062B041 clears EDX.
0x14062B055 jumps to 0x14062C8B0.
The same caller builds missing per-entry requests by calling 0x14062B170 when [entry+0x70] is null.

behavior: Loads begin from [vector+0] into RBX and end from [vector+0x8] into RBP.
Returns immediately when begin equals end.
For each entry, reads request = [entry+0x70], reads the request refcount at [request+0], decrements the count, and treats values <= 1 after decrement as completed/removable.
If all entries are still live, returns through the common epilogue at 0x14062CA67.
notes: The lack of a null check in this scan matches the caller contract: the tail-jump path is reached after 0x14062B014 has built requests for entries whose +0x70 pointer was missing.

behavior: Uses RBX as the write cursor at the first completed entry and R14/RDI as the read cursor at the next entry.
Skips read-side entries whose request refcount has completed.
For still-live read-side entries, copies the selector byte from [read_entry+0] to [write_entry+0].
Copies the string-like field at +0x08 through helper 0x1400B2490(write_entry+0x08, read_entry+0x08).
Moves the read-side request pointer from [read_entry+0x70] into [write_entry+0x70], zeros the read-side slot, and cleans/frees the overwritten write-side request pointer if present.
Advances write and read cursors by the 0x78-byte entry stride.

behavior_phases.phase: release discarded tail and adjust vector end
behavior: Calls 0x140172E60 with RCX = new_end and RDX = old_end to release the discarded tail range.
Computes the erased byte count as a multiple of the 0x78-byte entry stride.
Updates [vector+0x8] by the computed multiple so the vector end points at the compacted end.
evidence: 0x14062CA19 loads old_end from [R13+0x8].
0x14062CA1D moves new_end into RCX.
0x14062CA4B multiplies the computed entry count by 0x78.
0x14062CA4F adds the adjustment to [R13+0x8].

behavior: Restores saved nonvolatile registers and returns.

evidence: 0x14062C920 reads [read_entry+0].
0x14062C92C writes the byte to [write_entry+0].
0x14062C9C0 and 0x14062C9CB perform the same operation in the tail cleanup/move loop.

entry_layout_delta.role: String-like field assigned from read entry to write entry during compaction.
evidence: 0x14062C924 computes RDX = read_entry + 0x08.
0x14062C928 computes RCX = write_entry + 0x08.
0x14062C92E calls 0x1400B2490 to assign the field.

entry_layout_delta.role: Per-entry request pointer moved or freed during compaction.
evidence: 0x14062C8D0 reads [entry+0x70] during the initial refcount scan.
0x14062C90C and 0x14062C910 compute write/read request-slot addresses.
0x14062C938 reads the read-side request pointer and 0x14062C93B zeros the read-side slot.
0x14062C942 reads the overwritten write-side pointer and 0x14062C945 stores the read-side pointer into the write-side slot.
0x14062C950 calls 0x14062B8C0 on the overwritten pointer and 0x14062C95D frees it with size 0x268.

helper_roles.role: Assigns/copies a string-like field from source to destination, handling the short/heap sentinel at +0x17 before copying through 0x14002F7C0.
evidence: Reads destination +0x17 and clears the destination heap bookkeeping or resets the short-string sentinel.
Zeros the selected destination storage byte before copy.
Reads source +0x17, chooses heap or inline source storage, then calls 0x14002F7C0(destination, source, length).
Called by the compactor for entry +0x08 fields.

helper_roles.role: Cleans a 0x268-byte request object before it is freed.
evidence: Called on overwritten request pointers before 0x1407B8478 receives the same pointer and EDX = 0x268.

helper_roles.role: Releases the discarded tail entries after compaction.
evidence: 0x14062CA20 calls it with RCX = compacted new_end and RDX = old_end.

opennxt_947_server_implications: The pending event queue is self-pruning. Once an event request's refcount falls to the completed threshold, the client removes that entry and keeps only still-live event requests.
Queued impression/click/dismissal telemetry entries are therefore not durable records. They are in-flight lifecycle holders tied to request completion, cleanup, and vector compaction.
A server or replay harness may see event requests remain queued during bootstrap/login teardown only while the underlying 0x268 request object is still externally live.
If OpenNXT instrumentation watches the root +0x70 range, shrinking or reordered entries after request submission are expected behavior rather than dropped evidence.

This compaction path reinforces the lifecycle model: build missing requests, compact out completed entries, mark/cancel live entries when the root active request gate clears, then wait/release during close or teardown.

reverser_tool_update.reason: Existing Reverser outputs were sufficient after switching branch-target discovery from decoded full-section scanning to the raw strategy.
reverser_tool_update.next_native_capability_candidate: A bounded decoded branch-target mode with a time/scan budget or automatic raw fallback would prevent broad decoded scans from timing out on full .text target searches.

next_targets: Trace 0x14062BCE0 request submission internals so request +0x224 = 5 and literal data pointer 0x140B9AC18 can be named.
Resolve semantic names for the event path components copied from root +0x18, global +0x48, entry +0x08, and root +0x30.
Map the close/flush indirect CALL R10 path in 0x14062AB70.
Promote a reusable format-call argument resolver into Reverser once another formatter-heavy path confirms the same need.
